reports.ocrRawText:
THYROID FUNCTION TEST
Laboratory Name: Diagnostic Health Services
Report Date: 04-Mar-2026
Patient Name: Rajesh Verma
Age: 58 | Gender: Male | Sample ID: LAB-2026-89456

TEST RESULTS:
Test Name                Unit        Result      Reference Range    Status
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TSH (Thyroid Stim. Hormon) mIU/L      2.4         0.4 - 4.0 mIU/L     NORMAL
Free T4                   pmol/L     12.8        9.0 - 18.0 pmol/L   NORMAL
Free T3                   pmol/L     4.2         2.6 - 5.7 pmol/L    NORMAL
Total T4                  nmol/L     98.5        64 - 154 nmol/L     NORMAL

INTERPRETATION:
Thyroid function is normal. No evidence of hypo- or hyperthyroidism.

extractedData.interpretation: Normal thyroid function. No hypo- or hyperthyroidism detected.

reports.aiSummary: Thyroid panel is completely normal. All hormone levels including TSH, Free T4, Free T3, and Total T4 are within reference ranges. No thyroid dysfunction detected. Patient does not require thyroid medication adjustment. Annual monitoring recommended.

reports.ocrRawText:
COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL & KIDNEY FUNCTION TEST
Laboratory: Metro Diagnostics Center
Report Date: 18-Feb-2026
Patient: Priya Nair | Age: 42 | Gender: Female | Ref: MET-2026-56234

KIDNEY FUNCTION MARKERS:

Creatinine                 mg/dL      1.3         0.6 - 1.2 mg/dL     HIGH ⚠️
BUN (Blood Urea Nitrogen) mg/dL      24          7 - 20 mg/dL        HIGH ⚠️
GFR (est.)                mL/min     58          >60 mL/min          LOW  ⚠️
Sodium                    mEq/L      138         136 - 145 mEq/L     NORMAL
Potassium                 mEq/L      4.1         3.5 - 5.0 mEq/L     NORMAL
Phosphorus                mg/dL      3.8         2.5 - 4.5 mg/dL     NORMAL

CLINICAL INTERPRETATION:
Mildly elevated creatinine and BUN with estimated GFR 58 mL/min/1.73m² suggest Stage 2-3A chronic kidney disease. Recommend consultation with nephrologist, dietary sodium restriction, and repeat testing in 3 months.

extractedData.interpretation: Stage 2-3A CKD suggested by elevated creatinine and BUN with reduced GFR. Requires specialist evaluation.

reports.aiSummary: Metabolic panel indicates Stage 2-3A chronic kidney disease with creatinine 1.3 mg/dL (elevated), BUN 24 mg/dL (elevated), and estimated GFR 58 mL/min. Kidney function is declining. Electrolytes remain balanced. Immediate consultation with nephrology recommended for management plan. Dietary sodium and protein restriction advised. Repeat testing in 3 months. Monitor for progression to Stage 4 CKD.

reports.ocrRawText:
DIABETES MANAGEMENT & GLUCOSE MONITORING PANEL
Lab: Apollo Diagnostics, Pune
Report Date: 22-Jan-2026
Patient: Vikram Singh | Age: 51 | Diabetes Duration: 8 years | Ref: DIAB-2026-34098

GLUCOSE CONTROL MARKERS:

Fasting Blood Glucose     mg/dL      156         70 - 100 mg/dL      HIGH ⚠️⚠️
Postprandial Glucose (2h) mg/dL      238         <140 mg/dL          CRITICAL ⚠️⚠️
HbA1c                     %          8.9         <5.7%               HIGH ⚠️⚠️
Glycated Albumin          %          28          <17%                HIGH ⚠️

LIPID PANEL (Related):
Total Cholesterol        mg/dL      245         <200 mg/dL          HIGH ⚠️
LDL Cholesterol          mg/dL      165         <100 mg/dL          HIGH ⚠️
HDL Cholesterol          mg/dL      32          >50 mg/dL           LOW  ⚠️
Triglycerides            mg/dL      285         <150 mg/dL          HIGH ⚠️

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
Poorly controlled diabetes with HbA1c 8.9% indicating average 3-month glucose >250 mg/dL equivalent. Significant dyslipidemia evident. Risk of microvascular (retinopathy, nephropathy) and macrovascular (CAD) complications increased. URGENT: Medication review, lifestyle modifications, and referral to endocrinologist recommended.

extractedData.interpretation: Poorly controlled diabetes with high complication risk. Immediate intervention needed.

reports.aiSummary: Diabetes is severely uncontrolled with HbA1c 8.9% (target<7%). Fasting glucose 156 mg/dL and post-meal glucose 238 mg/dL indicate need for medication intensification. Accompanying dyslipidemia (LDL 165, triglycerides 285) significantly increases cardiovascular risk. Patient requires urgent endocrinologist consultation for therapy adjustment, continuous glucose monitoring consideration, and intensive lifestyle intervention. Retinopathy and nephropathy screening recommended as complications may be present.
